repository.name: ateam-mcp
repository.description: ADAS MCP Server — build, validate, and deploy multi-agent solutions from any AI environment

commit.message:
Fix bench-app: defer __TAURI__ access to avoid white screen

The script was accessing window.__TAURI__.core at parse time, before
Tauri had injected it. This caused an immediate TypeError that killed
the entire script block, resulting in a white window.

Fix: wrap tauriInvoke as a function that accesses __TAURI__ lazily,
and poll for its availability before auto-running the benchmark.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: switch to implicit OAuth flow to bypass PKCE verifier issue

The PKCE code verifier cookie was being lost during the Google OAuth
redirect chain. Implicit flow returns the token in the URL hash
fragment instead, eliminating the need for a code verifier entirely.

- Set flowType: "implicit" on browser Supabase client
- Callback page uses onAuthStateChange to detect session from hash

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v2.0.0: #[conduit::command] with full #[tauri::command] parity, backward-compatible refactoring

Major release adding the #[conduit::command] attribute macro with full feature parity
to #[tauri::command]: named parameters, State<T>/AppHandle/Window/Webview injection,
Result<T, E> returns, and truly async handlers (tokio-spawned). Includes backward-
compatible API refactoring (handler_raw() for v1 migration), new Queue/ChannelBuffer
types for reliable streaming, comprehensive documentation updates, and fresh benchmarks.

Breaking changes:
- .handler() accepts impl ConduitHandler (use handler_raw() for old closures)
- RingBuffer::push() returns usize (use push_checked() for PushOutcome)
- Parameter names stay snake_case in JSON (no camelCase conversion)
- Error::Serialize wraps sonic_rs::Error directly

See CHANGELOG.md for the complete list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Restore transparent tab bar content visibility with has_scrollback guard

Content was always drawn below the tab bar after 0c31082, breaking the
translucent overlay effect. Restore the has_scrollback condition so
content starts at y=0 only when the screen is full, and prefer
PostMultiplied surface alpha mode for correct compositing.

commit.message:
fix: test fixes — nav conda/ROS2 conflict, planner API, Vulkan soft-check

- test_navigation.py: use /usr/bin/python3 + _clean_env_for_ros() to
  avoid conda libtinfo.so.6 conflict with ROS2 rclpy (fixes 3 failures)
- test_teleop.py: update build_planner_message() call for new API
  (mode=int, movement/facing=3D vectors) (fixes 1 failure)
- test_build.py: Vulkan ICD + libnvidia-gl now soft-pass at build time

commit.message:
feat(extension): auto-start dashboard API server on activation

The web dashboard at immorterm.com/dashboard requires a local server
on port 3847 to access session data. Previously users had to manually
run `npx immorterm serve` — now the extension auto-starts it as a
detached process, matching the pattern used for the memory service.

commit.message:
fix: separate Telegram remote session from CLI node session

Telegram remote uses `tgremote:{node_id}` chat_id instead of
`node:{node_id}:{cwd_hash}`, so it never shares conversation
with CLI remote node sessions on the same machine.

commit.message:
fix(daemon): suppress shell startup OSC title changes with 2s grace period

Shells (zsh, oh-my-zsh) emit OSC 0 "zsh" during startup, overwriting
the user-facing "immorterm-#" display name. Add a 2-second grace period
after daemon spawn where OSC title changes are tracked but not broadcast
to WebSocket clients or persisted to the registry.
